PLAN.md — Bus Schedule & Class Reminder App
1. App Overview
A mobile app for university/college students that lets them store their class timetable and personal bus schedules in one place. The app automatically sends reminder notifications before each class or bus departure, helping students avoid being late. It is designed to work fully offline with no internet connection required.

2. Key Screens / Views
🏠 Home Screen

Displays today's upcoming classes and bus times in chronological order
Shows a countdown or time remaining until the next event
Quick-access button to add a new schedule entry

📅 Class Schedule Screen

List of all saved classes (subject name, time, day of week, room/location)
Tap any class to edit or delete it
"Add Class" button opens a form to input class details
Toggle to enable/disable reminder for each class

🚌 Bus Schedule Screen

List of saved bus routes (route name/number, departure time, days active)
Tap any entry to edit or delete
"Add Bus" button opens a form to input bus details
Toggle to enable/disable reminder for each bus entry

➕ Add / Edit Entry Screen (shared form)

Input fields: title, day(s) of week, time, location (optional), reminder lead time (e.g. 5, 10, 15, 30 mins before)
Save and Cancel buttons

🔔 Notification Settings Screen

Global toggle to enable/disable all reminders
Default reminder lead time setting
Option to set a custom notification sound (optional stretch goal)


3. Core Features

Add, edit, and delete class and bus schedule entries
Day-of-week repeating schedules (e.g. every Monday and Wednesday)
Local push notifications sent X minutes before each event
Today's view showing only the current day's relevant entries sorted by time
Per-entry reminder toggle to mute specific classes or bus routes without deleting them
Persistent local storage so all data survives app restarts


4. Data
What is stored:
FieldTypeDescriptionidInteger (auto)Unique ID for each entrytypeString"class" or "bus"titleStringSubject name or bus route namedaysStringComma-separated days (e.g. "MON,WED,FRI")timeStringHH:MM format (24hr)locationStringRoom number or bus stop (optional)reminder_minutesIntegerHow many minutes before to notifyis_activeBooleanWhether reminder is enabled
Where it is stored:

SQLite local database on the device using Android's built-in Room library
No cloud storage or internet connection needed
Data stays on the user's phone only


5. External Dependencies
DependencyPurposeRequired?Android Room (SQLite)Local data storage✅ Yes — core featureAndroid AlarmManager / WorkManagerScheduling and triggering notifications✅ Yes — core featureAndroid NotificationManagerDisplaying push notifications✅ Yes — core featureTransit/Bus API (e.g. local transit authority)Real-time bus data❌ No — users input times manuallyFirebase / Backend serverCloud sync or multi-user features❌ No — fully offline app

All dependencies are built into the Android SDK or available as standard Jetpack libraries. No external accounts, API keys, or internet access are required to build or run this app.


🗓️ Quick Timeline Reference
PhaseWeeksGoalSetup & PlanningWeek 1–2Android Studio setup, project structure, DB schemaCore BackendWeek 3–4Room database, alarm scheduling logicUI DevelopmentWeek 5–6All screens built and connectedIntegrationWeek 7–8Notifications firing, data persisting correctlyTesting & PolishWeek 9–10Bug fixes, UI cleanup, final demo prep
Team size: 1–3 members | Deadline: End of April

    32→@Composable
    33→fun HomeScreen(
    34→    onNavigateToAddEdit: () -> Unit = {},
    35→    onNavigateToNotifications: () -> Unit = {},
    36→    onCourseClick: (HomeEntry) -> Unit = {},
    37→    onEditEntry: (String) -> Unit = {}
    38→) {
    39→    val context = LocalContext.current
    40→    val vm: HomeEntryViewModel = viewModel(factory = HomeEntryViewModel.Factory(context))
    41→    val allEntries by vm.entries.collectAsState()
    42→
    43→    val workRepo = remember { WorkRepository.getInstance(context) }
    44→    val allWorks by workRepo.getAll().collectAsState()
    45→
    46→    val today = LocalDate.now()
    47→    val dateFormatter = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("EEEE, MMMM d", java.util.Locale.ENGLISH)
    48→    val formattedDate = today.format(dateFormatter)
    49→    val todayShort = today.dayOfWeek.getDisplayName(TextStyle.SHORT, Locale.ENGLISH)
    50→
    51→    // Live clock — refreshes every 30 seconds
    52→    var currentTime by remember { mutableStateOf(LocalTime.now()) }
    53→    LaunchedEffect(Unit) {
    54→        while (true) {
    55→            delay(30_000L)
    56→            currentTime = LocalTime.now()
    57→        }
    58→    }
    59→
    60→    // Filter to today's entries, sorted by time
    61→    val todayEntries = remember(allEntries, todayShort) {
    62→        allEntries
    63→            .filter { todayShort in it.daysOfWeek }
    64→            .sortedBy { it.time }
    65→    }
    66→
    67→    val pastEntries = todayEntries.filter { entryTime(it).isBefore(currentTime) }
    68→    val upcomingEntries = todayEntries.filter { !entryTime(it).isBefore(currentTime) }
    69→    val nextEntry = upcomingEntries.firstOrNull()
    70→
    71→    var bannerDismissed by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
    72→    // Reset dismiss when a new event becomes "next" so the banner reappears
    73→    LaunchedEffect(nextEntry?.title) { bannerDismissed = false }
    74→
    75→    // Incomplete Work items due today or overdue, sorted with oldest first
    76→    val dueWorks = remember(allWorks, today) {
    77→        allWorks
    78→            .filter { !it.isCompleted && DateUtils.isDueOrOverdue(it.dueDate, today) }
    79→            .sortedBy { it.dueDate }
    80→    }
    81→
    82→    // Incomplete Work items due in the next 7 days (strictly after today)
    83→    val upcomingWorks = remember(allWorks, today) {
    84→        allWorks
    85→            .filter { !it.isCompleted && DateUtils.isDueWithinDays(it.dueDate, today, 7) }
    86→            .sortedBy { it.dueDate }
    87→    }
    88→
    89→    Box(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize()) {
    90→        LazyColumn(
    91→            modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize(),
    92→            contentPadding = PaddingValues(horizontal = 16.dp, vertical = 16.dp),
    93→        ) {
    94→            // ── Header ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    95→            item {
    96→                Text(
    97→                    text = formattedDate,
    98→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.headlineSmall,
    99→                    fontWeight = FontWeight.SemiBold
   100→                )
   101→                Spacer(Modifier.height(4.dp))
   102→                val statusLine = homeStatusLine(allEntries.size, upcomingEntries.size)
   103→                Text(
   104→                    text = statusLine,
   105→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyLarge,
   106→                    color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
   107→                )
   108→                Spacer(Modifier.height(20.dp))
   109→            }
   110→
   111→            // ── Next Event Banner ─────────────────────────────────────────────
   112→            item {
   113→                if (nextEntry != null && !bannerDismissed) {
   114→                    NextEventBanner(
   115→                        entry = nextEntry,
   116→                        currentTime = currentTime,
   117→                        onDismiss = { bannerDismissed = true }
   118→                    )
   119→                } else if (nextEntry == null) {
   120→                    NoMoreEventsCard(isEmpty = allEntries.isEmpty())
   121→                }
   122→                Spacer(Modifier.height(20.dp))
   123→            }
   124→
   125→            // ── Today's upcoming entries ──────────────────────────────────────
   126→            item {
   127→                Text(
   128→                    text = "Today's Schedule",
   129→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium,
   130→                    fontWeight = FontWeight.SemiBold
   131→                )
   132→                Spacer(Modifier.height(8.dp))
   133→            }
   134→
   135→            if (upcomingEntries.isEmpty() && pastEntries.isEmpty()) {
   136→                item {
   137→                    Text(
   138→                        text = "Nothing scheduled for today — tap + to add an entry.",
   139→                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium,
   140→                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   141→                        modifier = Modifier.padding(vertical = 4.dp)
   142→                    )
   143→                }
   144→            } else if (upcomingEntries.isEmpty()) {
   145→                item {
   146→                    Text(
   147→                        text = "All done for today!",
   148→                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium,
   149→                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   150→                        modifier = Modifier.padding(vertical = 4.dp)
   151→                    )
   152→                }
   153→            } else {
   154→                items(upcomingEntries, key = { it.id }) { entry ->
   155→                    EntryListItem(
   156→                        entry = entry,
   157→                        isPast = false,
   158→                        onClick = { if (!entry.isBus) onCourseClick(entry) },
   159→                        onEdit = { onEditEntry(entry.id) }

   166→            if (pastEntries.isNotEmpty()) {

   207→
   208→            // ── Upcoming assignments (due in next 7 days) ─────────────────────

   219→                items(upcomingWorks, key = { "upcoming_${it.id}" }) { work ->

   229→            item { Spacer(Modifier.height(72.dp)) }

   244→@Composable
   245→private fun DueWorkItem(work: Work, today: LocalDate, onMarkDone: () -> Unit) {
   246→    val workDate = DateUtils.parseIsoDate(work.dueDate)
   247→    val isOverdue = workDate != null && workDate.isBefore(today)
   248→
   249→    Card(

   251→        shape = RoundedCornerShape(12.dp),
   252→        colors = CardDefaults.cardColors(
   253→            containerColor = if (isOverdue)
   254→                MaterialTheme.colorScheme.errorContainer.copy(alpha = 0.5f)
   255→            else
   256→                MaterialTheme.colorScheme.surface
   257→        ),
   258→        elevation = CardDefaults.cardElevation(defaultElevation = if (isOverdue) 0.dp else 2.dp)
   259→    ) {
   260→        Row(
   261→            modifier = Modifier
   262→                .fillMaxWidth()

content.text:
Here's what your app (**My Schedule**) does:

**Core concept:** A daily planner for university students that combines class schedules, bus schedules, and assignment tracking in one offline-first app.

**Screens:**
- **Home** — Today's classes and bus routes sorted by time, a "next event" countdown banner (turns red when ≤30 min away), overdue/upcoming assignment cards, and a live clock
- **Classes** — Manage recurring class entries (repeating by day-of-week)
- **Bus** — Manage saved bus trips with reminder toggles
- **Add/Edit** — Shared form for adding or editing either type of entry
- **Weather** — Weather info screen
- **Notifications** — Global reminder settings
- **AI Assistant** — An AI chat screen; also embedded in search results as an "Ask" card

**Cross-cutting features:**
- Global search that covers both schedule entries and assignments
- Assignment tracking with due dates and a "mark done" button
- Push notification reminders via AlarmManager, including a BootReceiver to reschedule after reboot
- Room database for all persistent data, fully offline

**Tech stack:** Kotlin, Jetpack Compose, Material Design 3, Room, AlarmManager, Navigation Compose, ViewModel/Flow architecture.

---

Before I review it — what would you consider a **successful version** of this app? That helps me know where to focus: polish, missing features, code quality, or something else?

   211→// ── Calendar card ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   212→@Composable
   213→private fun CalendarCard(
   214→    currentMonth: YearMonth,
   215→    selectedDate: LocalDate?,
   216→    dateEntryMap: Map<LocalDate?, List<ScheduleEntry>>,
   217→    onMonthChange: (YearMonth) -> Unit,
   218→    onDateSelected: (LocalDate) -> Unit
   219→) {
   220→    val today = LocalDate.now()
   221→    val firstDay = currentMonth.atDay(1)
   222→    val daysInMonth = currentMonth.lengthOfMonth()
   223→    // Sunday = 0 offset
   224→    val startOffset = (firstDay.dayOfWeek.value % 7)
   225→    val totalCells = startOffset + daysInMonth
   226→
   227→    Card(
   228→        modifier = Modifier
   229→            .fillMaxWidth()
   230→            .padding(horizontal = 12.dp, vertical = 8.dp),
   231→        shape = RoundedCornerShape(16.dp),
   232→        elevation = CardDefaults.cardElevation(4.dp),
   233→        colors = CardDefaults.cardColors(containerColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.surface)
   234→    ) {
   235→        Column(modifier = Modifier.padding(12.dp)) {
   236→
   237→            // Month navigation
   238→            Row(
   239→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   240→                horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.SpaceBetween,
   241→                verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically
   242→            ) {
   243→                IconButton(onClick = { onMonthChange(currentMonth.minusMonths(1)) }) {
   244→                    Icon(Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.ArrowBack, "Previous month")
   245→                }
   246→                Text(
   247→                    text = currentMonth.format(DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("MMMM yyyy", java.util.Locale.ENGLISH)),
   248→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium,
   249→                    fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold
   250→                )
   251→                IconButton(onClick = { onMonthChange(currentMonth.plusMonths(1)) }) {
   252→                    Icon(Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.ArrowForward, "Next month")
   253→                }
   254→            }
   255→
   256→            // Day-of-week headers
   257→            Row(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth()) {
   258→                listOf("S","M","T","W","T","F","S").forEach { day ->
   259→                    Text(
   260→                        text = day,
   261→                        modifier = Modifier.weight(1f),
   262→                        textAlign = TextAlign.Center,
   263→                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelSmall,
   264→                        fontWeight = FontWeight.SemiBold,
   265→                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
   266→                    )
   267→                }
   268→            }
   269→
   270→            Spacer(Modifier.height(4.dp))
   271→
   272→            // Day grid
   273→            val rows = (totalCells + 6) / 7
   274→            for (row in 0 until rows) {
   275→                Row(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth()) {
   276→                    for (col in 0 until 7) {
   277→                        val cellIndex = row * 7 + col
   278→                        val dayNumber = cellIndex - startOffset + 1
   279→                        if (dayNumber < 1 || dayNumber > daysInMonth) {
   280→                            Box(modifier = Modifier.weight(1f).aspectRatio(1f))
   281→                        } else {
   282→                            val date = currentMonth.atDay(dayNumber)
   283→                            val entriesOnDay = dateEntryMap[date] ?: emptyList()
   284→                            val isToday = date == today
   285→                            val isSelected = date == selectedDate
   286→
   287→                            DayCell(
   288→                                day = dayNumber,
   289→                                isToday = isToday,
   290→                                isSelected = isSelected,
   291→                                entries = entriesOnDay,
   292→                                modifier = Modifier.weight(1f),
   293→                                onClick = { onDateSelected(date) }
   294→                            )
   295→                        }
   296→                    }
   297→                }
   298→            }
   299→
   300→            // Legend
   301→            Spacer(Modifier.height(8.dp))
   302→            HorizontalDivider()
   303→            Spacer(Modifier.height(6.dp))
   304→            Row(
   305→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   306→                horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.SpaceEvenly
   307→            ) {
   308→                EntryType.entries.forEach { type ->
   309→                    Row(
   310→                        verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically,
   311→                        horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(4.dp)
   312→                    ) {
   313→                        Box(modifier = Modifier
   314→                            .size(8.dp)
   315→                            .clip(CircleShape)
   316→                            .background(entryColor(type)))
   317→                        Text(entryLabel(type),
   318→                            style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelSmall,
   319→                            color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)
   320→                    }
   321→                }
   322→            }
   323→        }
   324→    }
   325→}
   326→
   327→@Composable
   328→private fun DayCell(
   329→    day: Int,
   330→    isToday: Boolean,
   331→    isSelected: Boolean,
   332→    entries: List<ScheduleEntry>,
   333→    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
   334→    onClick: () -> Unit
   335→) {
   336→    val dotTypes = entries.map { it.type }.distinct().take(3)
   337→
   338→    Box(
   339→        modifier = modifier
   340→            .aspectRatio(0.72f)
   341→            .clip(RoundedCornerShape(6.dp))
   342→            .then(
   343→                if (isSelected) Modifier.background(MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary)
   344→                else if (isToday) Modifier.border(1.5.dp, MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary, RoundedCornerShape(6.dp))
   345→                else Modifier
   346→            )
   347→            .clickable(onClick = onClick)
   348→    ) {
   349→        // Day number centered
   350→        Text(
   351→            text = day.toString(),
   352→            fontSize = 13.sp,
   353→            fontWeight = if (isToday || isSelected) FontWeight.Bold else FontWeight.Normal,
   354→            color = if (isSelected) Color.White
   355→                    else if (isToday) MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary
   356→                    else MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurface,
   357→            modifier = Modifier.align(Alignment.Center)
   358→        )
   359→
   360→        // Colored indicator bars pinned to the bottom
   361→        if (dotTypes.isNotEmpty()) {
   362→            Row(
   363→                modifier = Modifier
   364→                    .align(Alignment.BottomCenter)
   365→                    .fillMaxWidth()
   366→                    .padding(horizontal = 3.dp, vertical = 3.dp),
   367→                horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(2.dp)
   368→            ) {
   369→                dotTypes.forEach { type ->
   370→                    Box(
   371→                        modifier = Modifier
   372→                            .weight(1f)
   373→                            .height(4.dp)
   374→                            .clip(RoundedCornerShape(2.dp))
   375→                            .background(
   376→                                if (isSelected) Color.White.copy(alpha = 0.85f)
   377→                                else entryColor(type)
   378→                            )
   379→                    )
   380→                }
   381→            }
   382→        }
   383→    }
   384→}

   471→@OptIn(ExperimentalMaterial3Api::class)
   472→@Composable
   473→private fun EntryDialog(
   474→    initial: ScheduleEntry?,
   475→    onDismiss: () -> Unit,
   476→    onConfirm: (ScheduleEntry) -> Unit
   477→) {
   478→    var type        by remember { mutableStateOf(initial?.type        ?: EntryType.COURSE) }
   479→    var title       by remember { mutableStateOf(initial?.title       ?: "") }
   480→    var description by remember { mutableStateOf(initial?.description ?: "") }
   481→    var date        by remember { mutableStateOf(initial?.date        ?: "") }
   482→    var time        by remember { mutableStateOf(initial?.time        ?: "") }
   483→    var location    by remember { mutableStateOf(initial?.location    ?: "") }
   484→    var showDatePicker by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
   485→
   486→    if (showDatePicker) {
   487→        val pickerState = rememberDatePickerState(
   488→            initialSelectedDateMillis = try {
   489→                LocalDate.parse(date).atStartOfDay().toInstant(ZoneOffset.UTC).toEpochMilli()
   490→            } catch (_: Exception) { null }
   491→        )
   492→        DatePickerDialog(
   493→            onDismissRequest = { showDatePicker = false },
   494→            confirmButton = {
   495→                TextButton(onClick = {
   496→                    val millis = pickerState.selectedDateMillis
   497→                    if (millis != null) {
   498→                        date = Instant.ofEpochMilli(millis)
   499→                            .atOffset(ZoneOffset.UTC).toLocalDate().toString()
   500→                    }
   501→                    showDatePicker = false
   502→                }) { Text("OK") }
   503→            },
   504→            dismissButton = {
   505→                TextButton(onClick = { showDatePicker = false }) { Text("Cancel") }

    17→class AgentViewModel(app: Application) : AndroidViewModel(app) {
    18→
    19→    private val db = AppDatabase.getInstance(app)
    20→
    21→    private val _messages = MutableStateFlow<List<ChatMessage>>(emptyList())
    22→    val messages: StateFlow<List<ChatMessage>> = _messages.asStateFlow()
    23→
    24→    private val _isLoading = MutableStateFlow(false)
    25→    val isLoading: StateFlow<Boolean> = _isLoading.asStateFlow()

    27→    fun send(userText: String) {
    28→        val trimmed = userText.trim()
    29→        if (trimmed.isBlank() || _isLoading.value) return
    30→
    31→        val history = _messages.value + ChatMessage("user", trimmed)
    32→        _messages.value = history
    33→        _isLoading.value = true
    34→
    35→        viewModelScope.launch {
    36→            val allEntries = db.homeEntryDao().getAllList()
    37→            val allWorks = db.workDao().getAllList()
    38→            val reply = withContext(Dispatchers.IO) {
    39→                try {

    41→                } catch (e: Exception) {
    42→                    "Error: ${e.message}"
    43→                }
    44→            }
    45→            _messages.value = _messages.value + ChatMessage("assistant", reply)
    46→            _isLoading.value = false
    47→        }
    48→    }
    49→

    51→        _messages.value = emptyList()
    52→    }

    37→private data class WeatherInfo(val emoji: String, val label: String)

    39→private fun weatherInfo(code: Int): WeatherInfo = when (code) {
    40→    0            -> WeatherInfo("☀️", "Clear Sky")
    41→    1            -> WeatherInfo("🌤️", "Mainly Clear")
    42→    2            -> WeatherInfo("⛅", "Partly Cloudy")
    43→    3            -> WeatherInfo("☁️", "Overcast")
    44→    in 45..48    -> WeatherInfo("🌫️", "Foggy")
    45→    in 51..55    -> WeatherInfo("🌦️", "Drizzle")
    46→    in 61..65    -> WeatherInfo("🌧️", "Rain")
    47→    in 71..77    -> WeatherInfo("❄️", "Snow")
    48→    in 80..82    -> WeatherInfo("🌦️", "Showers")
    49→    in 85..86    -> WeatherInfo("❄️", "Snow Showers")
    50→    95           -> WeatherInfo("⛈️", "Thunderstorm")
    51→    in 96..99    -> WeatherInfo("⛈️", "Thunderstorm")
    52→    else         -> WeatherInfo("🌡️", "Unknown")
    53→}

    55→/** "2026-04-13T14:00" → "2 PM" */
    56→private fun formatHour(isoDateTime: String): String {
    57→    return try {
    58→        val time = LocalTime.parse(isoDateTime.substring(11, 16))
    59→        time.format(DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("h a", Locale.ENGLISH))
    60→    } catch (_: Exception) { isoDateTime.substring(11, 16) }
    61→}

content.thinking:
Now I have a thorough picture of the app. Let me write a comprehensive review covering:

1. **Feature design**
2. **Code quality**
3. **Polish**

Let me organize my thoughts:

## Feature Design

**What works well:**
- The home screen is genuinely useful - today's schedule, countdown banner, assignments due today/upcoming week
- "Next event" banner with urgency (turns red at 30 mins) is smart UX
- Past entries greyed out is good
- Search that crosses schedule + assignments is well-thought-out
- AI assistant contextually loaded with user's schedule is a nice touch


- Suggestion chips in the empty AI state guide users nicely, and the reminder toggles per entry plus global notification settings give good control
- The boot receiver handling alarm rescheduling after reboot shows attention to detail

**Feature design issues:**

The app has two conflicting data models for classes that'll confuse users - `HomeEntry` handles recurring weekly schedules with a bus flag, while `ScheduleEntry` manages one-time dated events as courses, assignments, or special events. This means users have both a separate Classes calendar screen and the ability to add classes to the home screen, creating unclear mental models about where things should go.

The bottom navigation is overcrowded with 7 items, which violates Material Design guidelines and makes everything cramped and hard to tap. The "+" button living in the nav bar is also unconventional - it should probably be a floating action button instead, and having it there creates confusion since the home screen already has a FAB for adding entries. Users end up with two different ways to add from home but only the nav bar option from other screens.

There's also a deeper structural problem: the ClassScheduleScreen and HomeScreen are completely siloed. Adding a class through the calendar doesn't show it in today's view, and vice versa - entries created on the home tab never appear in the Classes calendar. This split will definitely confuse users since they're managing the same schedule in two disconnected places. CourseDetail for tracking assignments is only reachable from HomeEntries, so if someone adds a course through the Classes screen, there's no way to access its assignment tracking. The Weather tab is also taking up permanent navigation real estate when it's somewhat tangential to a schedule app's core purpose.

Looking at the code quality, the architecture is solid with clean MVVM and Repository pattern, Room persistence, StateFlow reactivity, and proper notification re-scheduling through BootReceiver. The validation logic is extracted into testable functions and visibility modifiers are used appropriately to keep helpers internal.

However, there are some threading issues I'm noticing. The `findById` call in CourseDetailScreen is being executed synchronously on the main thread when it should be a blocking Room query wrapped with `@WorkerThread` or converted to a suspend function. Similarly, the WorkRepository's `update` method is being called directly from the Composable in HomeScreen, which bypasses the ViewModel layer entirely—these repository operations should flow through the ViewModel instead.

There's also a pattern violation where HomeScreen is accessing both the HomeEntryViewModel and WorkRepository directly, mixing concerns that should be consolidated in a single ViewModel. The ViewModelFactory's `@Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")` annotations are unavoidable boilerplate, though some factories could use better documentation. The reminder options are hardcoded without a 1-hour option, which is a common user expectation. The time field in ClassScheduleScreen's EntryDialog also appears to have some flexibility issues that need addressing. the same value (line 358) — I'm calling `formatDayLabel` twice on the same date when determining font weight, which is wasteful. I should extract that to a variable instead. `AgentViewModel` also doesn't persist chat history, so restarting the app clears everything, though that's acceptable for a student project. The `clearHistory` function is essentially a no-op after process restart since messages aren't persisted anyway. On the positive side, the app handles empty states well, uses color changes for urgency (banner shifts at 30 minutes), and greys out past entries appropriately.

Looking at the polish issues now — the "DDL" label is jargon that should probably say "Assignment" instead. Bus entries on the home screen are clickable but do nothing when tapped, which is a dead interaction with no visual feedback to indicate they're not actionable. The edit icon is always visible on home screen entries, which might be confusing.

I'm also noticing inconsistencies: "Works & Assignments" should just be "Assignments," the calendar day headers use "S M T W T F S" where the two Ts are indistinguishable, the time field in the entry dialog is free-text while other time inputs use a TimePicker, and the empty state messages are inconsistent across different screens.

There's also a usability gap where assignment search results aren't clickable even though class entries are, the bottom navigation is cramped with seven items and an action button mixed in as a destination, and the chat history in AgentScreen disappears if the app is killed with no warning to the user.

content.text:
Here's my full review across three areas:

---

## Feature Design

**What's working well:**
- The Home screen is genuinely useful — countdown banner, urgency color at 30 min, greyed-out past entries, and "due today / upcoming 7 days" all make it feel like a real daily dashboard.
- The AI assistant being seeded with the user's actual schedule data is smart. Suggestion chips on the empty state are good onboarding.
- Boot receiver that re-schedules alarms after reboot is a thoughtful edge case most students miss.

**Issues:**

**1. Two disconnected class data models.** This is the most significant design problem. You have `HomeEntry` (recurring weekly schedule, daysOfWeek) and `ScheduleEntry` (one-time dated entries in the Classes tab, with type COURSE/ASSIGNMENT/SPECIAL_EVENT). These are entirely separate databases. Adding a class to the Home screen never makes it appear in the calendar. Adding an entry to the calendar never shows on the home screen. A user trying to track "CS 124 Lecture" would have to add it in two places for it to work in both views — and they'd have no idea that's required. These two models should either be unified or one should clearly serve the other.

**2. Bus entries are silently non-interactive on the Home screen.** `onClick = { if (!entry.isBus) onCourseClick(entry) }` — tapping a bus card does nothing, with no visual cue to the user. Either bus entries should open a detail/edit screen, or they shouldn't appear tappable. Right now it's a dead interaction.

**3. Seven bottom nav items is too many.** Material Design recommends 3–5. With Home, Classes, Bus, +, Weather, Reminders, and AI all in the nav bar, labels are cramped on small phones and the purpose of each isn't obvious at a glance. Suggestions:
- Weather could be a compact widget or card on the Home screen instead of its own tab.
- "Add/Edit" is an action, not a destination — it shouldn't live in the nav bar alongside screens. The FAB on Home is the right pattern; the nav bar item creates confusion (and a duplicate entry point from Home).
- Notifications/Reminders could live as a settings icon in the top bar.

**4. Assignment tracking is only reachable through class entries.** `CourseDetailScreen` is only accessible by tapping a HomeEntry on the Home screen. Classes added via the Classes calendar tab have no path to CourseDetail. And the search overlay links to `CourseDetailScreen` for entries — but assignment (`WorkSearchItem`) results aren't tappable at all. Users find their assignment in search and can't navigate to it.

---

## Code Quality

**What's solid:**
- Clean MVVM with Repository pattern throughout.
- StateFlow + `collectAsState`/`collectAsStateWithLifecycle` used correctly.
- Validation logic extracted to `internal` functions (`validateEntry`, `homeStatusLine`, etc.) — testable and clean.
- No hardcoded secrets visible.

**Issues:**

**1. `HomeEntryRepository.findById` called on the main thread** (`CourseDetailScreen.kt:38`):
```kotlin
val entry = remember { HomeEntryRepository.getInstance(context).findById(entryId) }
```
If this is a blocking Room query, it's a main-thread violation. Room enforces this at runtime with a crash in debug builds. It should be loaded via a ViewModel and exposed as a Flow or StateFlow.

**2. `WorkRepository` called directly from a Composable** (`HomeScreen.kt:202`):
```kotlin
onMarkDone = { workRepo.update(work.copy(isCompleted = true)) }
```
Repository calls should go through a ViewModel. If `update` launches a coroutine, this is fine — but if it's a suspend function or blocking call, this is incorrect. The HomeScreen also accesses `WorkRepository` directly alongside `HomeEntryViewModel`, which breaks MVVM consistency.

**3. Time field in the ClassScheduleScreen dialog is free text** (`ClassScheduleScreen.kt:552`). Everywhere else (AddEditEntryScreen) you use a proper `TimePicker`. Here the user can type "pizza" as a time and it will be saved with no validation. This should either use a TimePicker or validate the input.

**4. `formatDayLabel` called twice for the same value** in `DailyRow` (`WeatherScreen.kt:358`):
```kotlin
fontWeight = if (formatDayLabel(day.date) == "Today") FontWeight.Bold else FontWeight.Normal,
```
The result of `formatDayLabel` is already used in the `Text` above — extract it to a `val`.

**5. Package-level functions that should be private** in `ClassScheduleScreen.kt`: `entryColor`, `entryContainerColor`, `entryLabel`, `entryIcon`, `parseDate`, `formatDisplay` are all package-accessible. `parseDate` and `formatDisplay` are also essentially duplicates of logic in `DateUtils`. Either move them to `DateUtils` or mark them `private`.

---

## Polish

**What's solid:**
- Empty states handled everywhere, with actionable text.
- The "STARTING SOON" vs "UP NEXT" banner states feel polished.
- Typing indicator in the chat is a nice touch.
- Delete confirmation dialogs exist where they should.

**Issues:**

**1. "DDL" as an assignment label** (`ClassScheduleScreen.kt:62`). This is deadline jargon — most users will find "Assignment" or "Due" more intuitive. Unless your target users are specifically used to "DDL", this is a clarity issue.

**2. "Works & Assignments"** (`CourseDetailScreen.kt:87`). "Works" is awkward in English. "Assignments" alone is standard and cleaner.

**3. Calendar day headers use "S M T W T F S"** — the two T's (Tuesday and Thursday) are indistinguishable. Standard alternatives: "Su Mo Tu We Th Fr Sa" or single letters with a different visual treatment for duplicates.

**4. Edit icon is always visible on every Home screen entry.** The faint pencil icon on each card is low-signal visual noise. Most apps reveal edit actions on long-press or put them inside a detail screen.

**5. Chat history is ephemeral** — the AI chat history lives only in the ViewModel and is gone when the process is killed. There's no indicator to the user of this. If history isn't meant to persist, that's fine, but a subtle note ("Chat history clears when you close the app") sets correct expectations. If it should persist, the messages need to be written to Room.

---

**Summary priority order:**
1. Fix the two disconnected class data models (or clearly document/separate their purposes for the user)
2. Make bus entries on Home tappable (or visually non-interactive)
3. Fix `findById` on main thread
4. Make `WorkSearchItem` tappable in search results
5. Fix time field validation in ClassScheduleScreen dialog
6. Reduce bottom nav to 5 items max

Want to tackle any of these?

Based on what I've read:

**University students**, specifically ones who commute to campus. The core combination — recurring class schedule, bus routes with reminders, assignment tracking with due dates — maps almost exactly to a student who takes the bus to class and needs to keep track of homework deadlines.

The UI details reinforce this: the example placeholders say "MATH 241 Lecture" and "Route 22 Illini" (an UIUC bus route), the location examples say "Altgeld Hall 141" (an UIUC building), and the PLAN.md explicitly says "university/college students."

More specifically, I'd guess the primary user is **you** — a student at UIUC who built this to solve your own daily problem of juggling class times, bus schedules, and assignment deadlines in one place.

Am I close?

package com.example.combined_schedule.ui.viewmodel

import android.content.Context
import androidx.lifecycle.ViewModel
import androidx.lifecycle.ViewModelProvider
import com.example.combined_schedule.data.EntryType
import com.example.combined_schedule.data.ScheduleEntry
import com.example.combined_schedule.data.ScheduleEntryRepository
import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.StateFlow

class ClassScheduleViewModel(private val repo: ScheduleEntryRepository) : ViewModel() {

    val entries: StateFlow<List<ScheduleEntry>> = repo.getAll()

    fun insert(entry: ScheduleEntry) = repo.insert(entry)
    fun update(entry: ScheduleEntry) = repo.update(entry)
    fun delete(entry: ScheduleEntry) = repo.delete(entry)

    class Factory(private val context: Context) : ViewModelProvider.Factory {
        @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
        override fun <T : ViewModel> create(modelClass: Class<T>): T =
            ClassScheduleViewModel(ScheduleEntryRepository.getInstance(context)) as T
    }
}
